default_outcome: no — when in doubt, do not feed; verify with a vet

nodes.outcome: no — toxic to dogs, never feed
nodes.critic_note: expert-sized set; unlisted toxins still return 'no' via the default

nodes.outcome: no — concentrated form multiplies toxin and fat dose per gram
nodes.gray_zone: concentrated/powdered forms are unsafe absent food-specific data

nodes.outcome: no — low-fat/sugar-free peanut butter may contain xylitol; check the label
nodes.critic_note: kept over overengineering_critic — the low-fat form is the xylitol proxy the source skill missed

nodes.condition: quantity_grams is not None and dog_weight_kg is not None and dog_weight_kg > 0 and quantity_grams / dog_weight_kg > 50
nodes.outcome: caution — large dose relative to body weight
nodes.critic_note: guard requires dog_weight_kg > 0 so a falsy/zero weight cannot skip it
nodes.gray_zone: 50 g/kg is a placeholder threshold — calibrate per food

nodes.condition: quantity_grams is not None and (dog_weight_kg is None or dog_weight_kg <= 0)
nodes.outcome: unknown — a valid dog weight is required to assess this quantity
nodes.critic_note: a zero/missing weight can't be dose-assessed — fall to unknown, never safe

nodes.outcome: yes — standard peanut butter is safe in moderation, if xylitol-free

nodes.critic_note: demoted from a hard rule to a gray zone — the source skill ratifies no safe foods
nodes.gray_zone: safe-list has no ratified examples in the source skill; user ratified a conservative whitelist at the gate

nodes.outcome: yes — apple flesh only; never the core or seeds
nodes.critic_note: own node — the cyanogenic core/seed caveat can't fold into generic safe-in-moderation
